author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

ccel.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

title: Matthew VI. 1.

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Take heed that ye do not your almsbefore men, to be seen of them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He roots out in what remains the most tyrannical passion of all, the rage and madness with respect to vainglory, which springs up in them that do right.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For at first He had not at all discoursed about it; it being indeed superfluous, before He had persuaded them to do any of the things which they ought, to teach in which way they should practise and pursue them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But after He had led them on to self-command, then He proceeds to purge away also the alloy which secretly subsists with it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this disease is by no means of random birth; but when we have duly performed many of the commandments.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It behooved therefore first to implant virtue, and then to remove the passion which mars its fruit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And see with what He begins, with fasting, and prayer, and almsgiving: for in these good deeds most especially it is wont to make its haunt.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The Pharisee, for instance, was hereby puffed up, who saith, “I fast twice a week, I give tithes of my substance.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And he was vainglorious too in his very prayer, making it for display.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For since there was no one else present, he pointed himself out to the publican,saying, “I am not as the rest of men, nor even as this publican.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And mark how Christ began, as though He were speaking of some wild beast, hard to catch, and crafty to deceive him who was not very watchful.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus, “take heed,” saith He, “as to your alms.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So Paul also speaks to the Philippians; “Beware of dogs.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And with reason, forthe evil beast comes in upon us secretly, and without noise puffs all away, and unobservedly carries out all that is within.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Forasmuch then as He had made much discourse about almsgiving, and brought forward God, “Who maketh His sun to rise on the evil and the good,”and by motives from all quarters had urged them on to this, and had persuaded them to exult in the abundance of their giving; He finishes by taking away also all things that encumber this fair olive tree.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For which same cause He saith, “Take heed that ye do not your alms before men,” for that which was before mentioned, is “God’s” almsgiving.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And when He had said, “not to do it before men,” He added, “to be seen of them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And though it seems as if the same thing were said a second time, yet if any one give particular attention, it is not the same thing, but one is different from the other; and it hath great security, and unspeakable care and tenderness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it may be, both that one doing alms before men may not do it to be seen of them, and again that one not doing it before men may do it to be seen of them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore it is not simply the thing, but the intent, which He both punishes and rewards.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And unless such exactness were employed, this would make many more backward about the giving of alms, because it is not on every occasion altogether possible to do it secretly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this cause, setting thee free from this restraint, He defines both the penalty and the reward not by the result of the action, but by the intention of the doer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That is, that thou mayest not say, “What? am I then the worse, should another see?”—“it is not this,” saith He, “that I am seeking, but the mind that is in thee, and the tone of what thou doest.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For His will is to bring our soul altogether into frame, and to deliver it from every disease.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now having, as you see, forbidden men’s acting for display, and having taught them the penalty thence ensuing, namely, to do it vainly, and for nought, He again rouses their spirits by putting them in mind of the Father, and of Heaven, that not by the loss alone He might sting them, but also shame them by the recollection of Him who gave them being.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For ye have no reward,” saith He, “with your Father which is in Heaven.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nor even at this did He stop, but proceeds yet further, by other motives also increasing their disgust.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For as above He set forth publicans and heathens, by the quality of the person shaming their imitators, so also in this place the hypocrites.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Therefore when thou doest thine alms,” saith He, “do not sound a trumpet before thee, as the hypocrites do.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Not that they had trumpets, but He means to display the greatness of their frenzy, by the use of this figure of speech, deriding and making a showof them hereby.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And well hath He called them “hypocrites” for the mask was of mercy, but the spirit of cruelty and inhumanity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they do it, not because they pity their neighbors, but that they themselves may enjoy credit; and this came of the utmost cruelty; while another was perishing with hunger, to be seeking vainglory, and not putting an end to his suffering.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It is not then the giving alms which is required, but the giving as one ought, the giving for such and such an end.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Having then amply derided those men, and having handled them so, that the hearer should be even ashamed of them, He again corrects thoroughly the mind which is so distempered: and having said how we ought not to act, He signifies on the other hand how we ought to act.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How then ought we to do our alms?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Let not thy left hand know,” saith He, “what thy right hand doeth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Here again His enigmatical meaning is not of the hands, but He hath put the thing hyperbolically.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: As thus: “If it can be,” saith He, “for thyself not to know it, let this be the object of thine endeavor; that, if it were possible, it may be concealed from the very hands that minister.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It is not, as some say, that we should hide it from wrong-headedmen, for He hath here commanded that it should be concealed from all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And then the reward too; consider how great it is.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For after He had spoken of the punishment from the one, He points out also the honor derived from the other; from either side urging them, and leading them on to high lessons.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, for He is persuading them to know that God is everywhere present, and that not by our present life are our interests limited, but a yet more awful tribunal will receive us when we go hence, and the account of all our doings, and honors, and punishments: and that no one will be hid in doing anything either great or small, though he seem to be hid from men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For all this did He darkly signify, when He said,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Thy Father which seeth in secret shall reward thee openly.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Setting for him a great and august assemblage of spectators, and what He desires, that very thing bestowing on him in great abundance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For what,” saith He, “dost thou wish? is it not to have some to be spectators of what is going on?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Behold then, thou hast some; not angels, nor archangels, but the God of all.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if thou desire to have men also as spectators, neither of this desire doth He deprive thee at the fitting season, but rather in greater abundance affords it unto thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, if thou shouldest now make a display, thou wilt be able to make it to ten only, or twenty, or (we will say) a hundred persons: but if thou take pains to lie hid now, God Himself will then proclaim thee in the presence of the whole universe.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore above all, if thou wilt have men see thy good deeds, hide them now, that then all may look on them with the more honor, God making them manifest, and extolling them, and proclaiming them before all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Again, whereas now they that behold will rather condemn thee as vainglorious; when they see thee crowned, so far from condemning, they will even admire thee, all of them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: When therefore by waiting a little, thou mayest both receive a reward, and reap greater admiration; consider what folly it is to cast thyself out of both these; and while thou art seeking thy reward from God, and while God is beholding, to summon men for the display of what is going on.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why, if display must be made of our love, to our Father above all should we make it; and this most especially, when our Father hath the power both to crown and to punish.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And let me add, even were there no penalty, it were not meet for him who desires glory, to let go this our theatre, and take in exchange that of men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For who is there so wretched, as that when the king was hastening to come and see his achievements, he would let him go, and make up his assembly of spectators of poor men and beggars?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this cause then, He not only commands to make no display, but even to take pains to be concealed: it not being at all the same, not to strive for publicity, and to strive for concealment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And when ye pray,” saith He, “ye shall not be as the hypocrites, for they love to pray standing in the synagogues, and in the corners of the streets.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Verily I say unto you, they have their reward.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But thou, when thou prayest, enter into thy closet, and when thou hast shut thy door, pray to thy Father which is in secret.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: These too again He calls “hypocrites,” and very fitly; for while they are feigning to pray to God, they are looking round after men; wearing the garb not of suppliants, but of ridiculous persons.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he, who is to do a suppliant’s office, letting go all other, looks to him alone, who hath power to grant his request.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if thou leave this one, and go about wandering and casting around thine eyes everywhere, thou wilt depart with empty hands.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this was thine own will.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore He said not, “such shall not receive a reward,” but, “they have it out:” that is, they shall indeed receive one, but from those of whom they themselves desire to have it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For God wills not this: He rather for His part was willing to bestow on men the recompence that comes from Himself; but they seeking that which is from men, can be no longer justly entitled to receive from Him, for whom they have done nothing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But mark, I pray thee, the lovingkindness of God, in that He promises to bestow on us a reward, even for those good things which we ask of Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Having then discredited them, who order not this duty as they ought, both from the place and from their disposition of mind, and having shown that they are very ridiculous: He introduces the best manner of prayer, and again gives the reward, saying, “Enter into thy closet.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “What then,” it may be said, “ought we not to pray in church?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Indeed we ought by all means, but in such a spirit as this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because everywhere God seeks the intention of all that is done.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since even if thou shouldest enter into thy closet, and having shut the door, shouldest do it for display, the doors will do thee no good.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It is worth observing in this case also, how exact the definition, which He made when He said, “That they may appear unto men.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that even if thou shut the doors, this He desires thee duly to perform, rather than the shutting of the doors, even to shut the doors of the mind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For as in everything it is good to be freed from vainglory, so most especially in prayer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if even without this, we wander and are distracted, when shall we attend unto the things which we are saying, should we enter in having this disease also?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if we who pray and beseech attend not, how do we expect God to attend?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But yet some there are, who after such and so earnest charges, behave themselves so unseemly in prayer, that even when their person is concealed, they make themselves manifest to all by their voice, crying out disorderly,and rendering themselves objects of ridicule both by gesture and voice.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou not that even in a market place, should any one come up doing like this, and begging clamorously, he wilt drive away him whom he is petitioning; but if quietly, and with the proper gesture, then he rather wins over him that can grant the favor?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us not then make our prayer by the gesture of our body, nor by the loudness of our voice, but by the earnestness of our mind: neither with noise and clamor and for display, so as even to disturb those that are near us, but with all modesty,and with contrition in the mind, and with inward tears.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But art thou pained in mind, and canst not help crying aloud? yet surely it is the part of one exceedingly pained to pray and entreat even as I have said.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since Moses too was pained, and prayed in this way and was heard; for this cause also God said unto him, “Wherefore criest thou unto me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And Hannah too again, her voice not being heard, accomplished all she wished, forasmuch as her heart cried out.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But Abel prayed not only when silent, but even when dying, and his blood sent forth a cry more clear than a trumpet.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Do thou also then groan, even as that holy one, I forbid it not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Rend,” as the prophet commanded,“thine heart, and not thy garments.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Out of deeps call upon God, for it is said, “Out of the depths have I cried to Thee, O Lord.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: From beneath, out of the heart, draw forth a voice, make thy prayer a mystery.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou not that even in the houses of kings all tumult is put away, and great on all sides is the silence?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Do thou also therefore, entering as into a palace,—not that on the earth, but what is far more awful than it, that which is in heaven,—show forth great seemliness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, for thou art joined to the choirs of angels, and art in communion with archangels, and art singing with the seraphim.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And all these tribes show forth much goodly order, singing with great awe that mystical strain, and their sacred hymns to God, the King of all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: With these then mingle thyself, when thou art praying, and emulate their mystical order.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not unto men art thou praying, but to God, who is everywhere present, who hears even before the voice, who knows the secrets of the mind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If thou so pray, great is the reward thou shalt receive.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For thy Father,” saith He, “who seeth in secret, shall reward thee openly.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He said not, “shall freely give thee,” but, “shall reward thee;” yea, for He hath made Himself a debtor to thee, and even from this hath honored thee with great honor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For because He Himself is invisible, He would have thy prayer be so likewise.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then He speaks even the very words of the prayer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “When ye pray,” saith He, “use no vain repetitions, even as the heathen do.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: You see that when He was discoursing of almsgiving, He removed only that mischief which comes of vainglory, and added nothing more; neither did He say whence one should give alms; as from honest labor, and not from rapine nor covetousness: this being abundantly acknowledged among all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And also before that, He had thoroughly cleared up this point, when He blessed them “that hunger after righteousness.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But touching prayer, He adds somewhat over and above; “not to use vain repetitions.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And as there He derides the hypocrites, so here the heathen; shaming the hearer everywhere most of all by the vileness of the persons.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For since this, in most cases, is especially biting and stinging, I mean our appearing to be likened to outcast persons; by this topic He dissuades them; calling frivolousness, here, by the name of “vain repetition:” as when we ask of God things unsuitable, kingdoms, and glory, and to get the better of enemies, and abundance of wealth, and in general what does not at all concern us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For He knoweth,” saith He, “what things ye have need of.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And herewith He seems to me to command in this place, that neither should we make our prayers long; long, I mean, not in time, but in the number and length of the things mentioned.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For perseverance indeed in the same requests is our duty: His word being, “continuing instant in prayer.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He Himself too, by that example of the widow, who prevailed with the pitiless and cruel ruler, by the continuance of her intercession;and by that of the friend, who came late at night time, and roused the sleeper from his bed,not for his friendship’s, but for his importunity’s sake; what did He, but lay down a law, that all should continually make supplication unto Him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He doth not however bid us compose a prayer of ten thousand clauses, and so come to Him and merely repeat it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this He obscurely signified when He said, “They think that they shall be heard for their much speaking.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For He knoweth,” saith He, “what things ye have need of.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if He know, one may say, what we have need of, wherefore must we pray?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Not to instruct Him, but to prevail with Him; to be made intimate with Him, by continuance in supplication; to be humbled; to be reminded of thy sins.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “After this manner, therefore, pray ye,” saith He: “Our Father, which art in heaven.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See how He straightway stirred up the hearer, and reminded him of all God’s bounty in the beginning.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he who calls God Father, by him both remission of sins, and taking away of punishment, and righteousness, and sanctification, and redemption, and adoption, and inheritance, and brotherhood with the Only-Begotten, and the supply of the Spirit, are acknowledged in this single title.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For one cannot call God Father, without having attained to all those blessings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Doubly, therefore, doth He awaken their spirit, both by the dignity of Him who is called on, and by the greatness of the benefits which they have enjoyed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But when He saith, “in Heaven,” He speaks not this as shutting up God there, but as withdrawing him who is praying from earth, and fixing him in the high places, and in the dwellings above.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He teaches, moreover, to make our prayer common, in behalf of our brethren also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He saith not, “my Father, which art in Heaven,” but, “our Father,” offering up his supplications for the body in common, and nowhere looking to his own, but everywhere to his neighbor’s good.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And by this He at once takes away hatred, and quells pride, and casts out envy, and brings in the mother of all good things, even charity, and exterminates the inequality of human things, and shows how far the equality reaches between the king and the poor man, if at least in those things which are greatest and most indispensable, we are all of us fellows.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what harm comes of our kindred below, when in that which is on high we are all of us knit together, and no one hath aught more than another; neither the rich more than the poor, nor the master than the servant, neither the ruler than the subject, nor the king than the common soldier, nor the philosopher than the barbarian, nor the skillful than the unlearned?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For to all hath He given one nobility, having vouchsafed to be called the Father of all alike.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: When therefore He hath reminded us of this nobility, and of the gift from above, and of our equality with our brethren, and of charity; and when He hath removed us from earth, and fixed us in Heaven; let us see what He commands us to ask after this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Not but, in the first place, even that saying alone is sufficient to implant instruction in all virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he who hath called God Father, and a common Father, would be justly bound to show forth such a conversation, as not to appear unworthy of this nobility, and to exhibit a diligence proportionate to the gift.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet is He not satisfied with this, but adds, also another clause, thus saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Hallowed be Thy name.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Worthy of him who calls God Father, is the prayer to ask nothing before the glory of His Father, but to account all things secondary to the work of praising Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For “hallowed” is glorified.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For His own glory He hath complete, and ever continuing the same, but He commands him who prays to seek that He may be glorified also by our life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Which very thing He had said before likewise, “Let your light so shine before men, that they may see your good works, and glorify your Father which is in heaven.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, and the seraphim too, giving glory, said on this wise, “Holy, holy, holy.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that “hallowed” means this, viz. “glorified.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That is, “vouchsafe,” saith he, “that we may live so purely, that through us all may glorify Thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Which thing again appertains unto perfect self-control, to present to all a life so irreprehensible, that every one of the beholders may offer to the Lord the praise due to Him for this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this again is the language of a right-minded child, not to be rivetted to things that are seen, neither to account things present some great matter; but to hasten unto our Father, and to long for the things to come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this springs out of a good conscience, and a soul set free from things that are on earth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This, for instance, Paul himself was longing after every day: wherefore he also said, that “even we ourselves, who have the first-fruits of the Spirit, groan, waiting for an adoption, the redemption of our body.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he who hath this fondness,can neither be puffed up by the good things of this life, nor abashed by its sorrows; but as though dwelling in the very heavens, is freed from each sort of irregularity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Thy will be done in earth, as it is in Heaven.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Behold a most excellent train of thought! in that He bade us indeed long for the things to come, and hasten towards that sojourn; and, till that may be, even while we abide here, so long to be earnest in showing forth the same conversation as those above.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For ye must long, saith He, for heaven, and the things in heaven; however, even before heaven, He hath bidden us make the earth a heaven and do and say all things, even while we are continuing in it, as having our conversation there; insomuch that these too should be objects of our prayer to the Lord.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For there is nothing to hinder our reaching the perfection of the powers above, because we inhabit the earth; but it is possible even while abiding here, to do all, as though already placed on high.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What He saith therefore is this: “As there all things are done without hindrance, and the angels are not partly obedient and partly disobedient, but in all things yield and obey (for He saith, ‘Mighty in strength, performing His word’);so vouchsafe that we men may not do Thy will by halves, but perform all things as Thou willest.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how He hath taught us also to be modest, by making it clear that virtue is not of our endeavors only, but also of the grace from above?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And again, He hath enjoined each one of us, who pray, to take upon himself the care of the whole world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He did not at all say, “Thy will be done” in me, or in us, but everywhere on the earth; so that error may be destroyed, and truth implanted, and all wickedness cast out, and virtue return, and no difference in this respect be henceforth between heaven and earth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For if this come to pass,” saith He, “there will be no difference between things below and above, separated as they are in nature; the earth exhibiting to us another set of angels.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Give us this day our daily bread.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What is “daily bread”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That for one day.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For because He had said thus, “Thy will be done in earth as it is in heaven,” but was discoursing to men encompassed with flesh, and subject to the necessities of nature, and incapable of the same impassibility with the angels:—while He enjoins the commands to be practised by us also, even as they perform them; He condescends likewise, in what follows, to the infirmity of our nature.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus, “perfection of conduct,” saith He, “I require as great, not however freedom from passions; no, for the tyranny of nature permits it not: for it requires necessary food.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But mark, I pray thee, how even in things that are bodily, that which is spiritual abounds.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it is neither for riches, nor for delicate living, nor for costly raiment, nor for any other such thing, but for bread only, that He hath commanded us to make our prayer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And for “daily bread,” so as not to “take thought for the morrow.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because of this He added, “daily bread,” that is, bread for one day.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And not even with this expression is He satisfied, but adds another too afterwards, saying, “Give us this day;” so that we may not, beyond this, wear ourselves out with the care of the following day.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For that day, the intervalbefore which thou knowest not whether thou shalt see, wherefore dost thou submit to its cares?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This, as He proceeded, he enjoined also more fully, saying, “Take no thought for the morrow.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He would have us be on every hand unencumbered and winged for flight, yielding just so much to nature as the compulsion of necessity requires of us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then forasmuch as it comes to pass that we sin even after the washing of regeneration, He, showing His love to man to be great even in this case, commands us for the remission of our sins to come unto God who loves man, and thus to say,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Forgive us our debts, as we also forgive our debtors.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou surpassing mercy?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: After taking away so great evils, and after the unspeakable greatness of His gift, if men sin again, He counts them such as may be forgiven.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For that this prayer belongs to believers, is taught us both by the laws of the church, and by the beginning of the prayer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the uninitiated could not call God Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If then the prayer belongs to believers, and they pray, entreating that sins may be forgiven them, it is clear that not even after the laver is the profit of repentance taken away.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since, had He not meant to signify this, He would not have made a law that we should so pray.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now He who both brings sins to remembrance, and bids us ask forgiveness, and teaches how we may obtain remission and so makes the way easy; it is perfectly clear that He introduced this rule of supplication, as knowing, and signifying, that it is possible even after the fontto wash ourselves from our offenses; by reminding us of our sins, persuading us to be modest; by the command to forgive others, setting us free from all revengeful passion; while by promising in return for this to pardon us also, He holds out good hopes, and instructs us to have high viewsconcerning the unspeakable mercy of God toward man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But what we should most observe is this, that whereas in each of the clauses He had made mention of the whole of virtue, and in this way had included also the forgetfulness of injuries (for so, that “His name be hallowed,” is the exactness of a perfect conversation; and that “His will be done,” declares the same thing again: and to be able to call God “Father,” is the profession of a blameless life; in all which things had been comprehended also the duty of remitting our anger against them that have transgressed): still He was not satisfied with these, but meaning to signify how earnest He is in the matter, He sets it down also in particular, and after the prayer, He makes mention of no other commandment than this, saying thus:

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For if ye forgive men their trespasses, your heavenly Father also will forgive you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that the beginning is of us, and we ourselves have control over the judgment that is to be passed upon us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in order that no one, even of the senseless, might have any complaint to make, either great or small, when brought to judgment; on thee, who art to give account, He causes the sentence to depend; and “in what way soever thou hast judged for thyself,in the same,” saith He, “do I also judge thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if thou forgive thy fellow servant, thou shalt obtain the same favor from me; though indeed the one be not equal to the other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For thou forgivest in thy need, but God, having need of none: thou, thy fellow slave; God, His slave: thou liable to unnumbered charges; God, being without sin.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But yet even thus doth He show forth His lovingkindness towards man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since He might indeed, even without this, forgive thee all thine offenses; but He wills thee hereby also to receive a benefit; affording thee on all sides innumerable occasions of gentleness and love to man, casting out what is brutish in thee, and quenching wrath, and in all ways cementing thee to him who is thine own member.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what canst thou have to say? that thou hast wrongfully endured some ill of thy neighbor?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: (For these only are trespasses, since if it be done with justice, the act is not a trespass.)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But thou too art drawing near to receive forgiveness for such things, and for much greater.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And even before the forgiveness, thou hast received no small gift, in being taught to have a human soul, and in being trained to all gentleness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And herewith a great reward shall also be laid up for thee elsewhere, even to be called to account for none of thine offenses.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What sort of punishment then do we not deserve, when after having received the privilege, we betray our salvation?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And how shall we claim to be heard in the rest of our matters, if we will not, in those which depend on us, spare our own selves?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And lead us not into temptation; but deliver us from the evil one: for Thine is the kingdom, and the power, and the glory, for ever.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Here He teaches us plainly our own vileness, and quells our pride, instructing us to deprecate all conflicts, instead of rushing upon them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For so both our victory will be more glorious, and the devil’s overthrow more to be derided.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I mean, that as when we are dragged forth, we must stand nobly; so when we are not summoned, we should be quiet, and wait for the time of conflict; that we may show both freedom from vainglory, and nobleness of spirit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He here calls the devil “the wicked one,” commanding us to wage against him a war that knows no truce, and implying that he is not such by nature.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For wickednessis not of those things that are from nature, but of them that are added by our own choice.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And he is so called pre-eminently, by reason of the excess of his wickedness, and because he, in no respect injured by us, wages against us implacable war.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore neither said He, “deliver us from the wicked ones,” but, “from the wicked one;” instructing us in no case to entertain displeasure against our neighbors, for what wrongs soever we may suffer at their hands, but to transfer our enmity from these to him, as being himself the cause of all our wrongs.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Having then made us anxious as before conflict, by putting us in mind of the enemy, and having cut away from us all our remissness; He again encourages and raises our spirits, by bringing to our remembrance the King under whom we are arrayed, and signifying Him to be more powerful than all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For Thine,” saith He, “is the kingdom, and the power, and the glory.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Doth it not then follow, that if His be the kingdom, we should fear no one, since there can be none to withstand, and divide the empire with him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when He saith, “Thine is the kingdom,” He sets before us even him, who is warring against us, brought into subjection, though he seem to oppose, God for a while permitting it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in truth he too is among God’s servants, though of the degraded class, and those guilty of offense; and he would not dare set upon any of his fellow servants, had he not first received license from above.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And why say I, “his fellow servants?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Not even against swine did he venture any outrage, until He Himself allowed him;nor against flocks, nor herds, until he had received permission from above.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And the power,” saith He.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore, manifold as thy weakness may be, thou mayest of right be confident, having such a one to reign over thee, who is able fully to accomplish all, and that with ease, even by thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And the glory, for ever.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus He not only frees thee from the dangers that are approaching thee, but can make thee also glorious and illustrious.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For as His power is great, so also is His glory unspeakable, and they are all boundless, and no end of them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how He hath by every means anointed His Champion, and hath framed Him to be full of confidence?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then, as I said before, meaning to signify, that of all things He most loathes and hates bearing malice, and most of all accepts the virtue which is opposite to that vice; He hath after the prayer also again put us in mind of this same point of goodness; both by the punishment set, and by the reward appointed, urging the hearer to obey this command.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For if ye forgive men,” saith He, “your heavenly Father will also forgive you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if ye forgive not, neither will He forgive you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: With this view He hath again mentioned heaven also, and their Father; to abash the hearer by this topic likewise; that he of all people, being of such a Father, should be made a wild beast of; and summoned as he is to heaven, should cherish an earthly and ordinarysort of mind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since not by grace only, you see, ought we to become His children, but also by our works.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And nothing makes us so like God, as being ready to forgive the wicked and wrong-doers; even as indeed He had taught before, when He spake of His “making the sun to shine on the evil and on the good.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this same cause again in every one of the clauses He commands us to make our prayers common, saying, “Our Father,” and “Thy will be done in earth as it is in heaven,” and “Give us the bread, and forgive us our debts,” and “lead us not into temptation,” and “deliver us;” everywhere commanding us to use this plural word, that we may not retain so much as a vestige of anger against our neighbor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How great punishment then must they deserve, who after all this, so far from themselves forgiving, do even entreat God for vengeance on their enemies, and diametrically as it were transgress this law; and this while He is doing and contriving all, to hinder our being at variance one with another?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For since love is the root of all that is good, He removing from all sides whatever mars it, brings us together, and cements us to each other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For there is not, there is not one, be he father, or mother, or friend, or what you will, who so loved us as the God who created us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this, above all things, both His daily benefits and His precepts make manifest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if thou tell me of the pains, and of the sorrows, and of the evils of life; consider in how many things thou offendest Him every day, and thou wilt no longer marvel, though more than these evils should come upon thee, but if thou shouldest enjoy any good, then thou wilt marvel, and be amazed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But as it is, we look upon the calamities that come upon us, but the offenses, whereby we offend daily, we consider not: therefore we are perplexed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since if we did but reckon up with strictness our sins of one day only, in that case we should know well how great evils we must be liable to.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And to let pass the other misdoings of which we have been guilty, each one for himself, and to speak of what have been committed this day; although of course I know not in what each of us may have sinned, yet such is the abundance of our misdoings, that not even he who knew all exactly would be able to choose from among these only.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Which of us, for instance, hath not been careless in his prayers?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Which hath not been insolent, or vainglorious?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Who hath not spoken evil of his brother, hath not admitted a wicked desire, hath not looked with unchaste eyes, hath not remembered things with hostile feeling, even till he made his heart swell?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if while we are in church, and in a short time we have become guilty of so great evils; what shall be when we are gone out from hence?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If in the harbor the waves are so high, when we are gone forth into the channel of wickednesses, the forum I mean, and to public business, and our cares at home, shall we indeed be able so much as to know ourselves again?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But yet from our so great and so many sins, God hath given us a short and easy way of deliverance, and one that is free from all toil.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what sort of toil is it to forgive him that hath grieved us?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, it is a toil not to forgive, but to keep up our enmity: even as to be delivered from the anger, both works in us a great refreshment, and is very easy to him that is willing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For there is no sea to be crossed, nor long journey to be travelled, nor summits of mountains to be passed over, nor money to be spent, no need to torment thy body; but it suffices to be willing only, and all our sins are done away.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if so far from forgiving him thyself, thou makest intercession to God against him, what hope of salvation wilt thou then have, if at the very time when thou oughtest rather to appease God, even then thou provokest Him; putting on the garb of a suppliant, but uttering the cries of a wild beast, and darting out against thyself those shafts of the wicked one?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore Paul also, making mention of prayer, required nothing so much as the observance of this commandment; for He saith, “lifting up holy hands without wrath and doubting.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if when thou hast need of mercy, not even then wilt thou let go thine anger, but art rather exceedingly mindful of it, and that, although thou knowest thou art thrusting the sword into thyself; when will it be possible for thee to become merciful, and to spew out the evil venom of this wickedness?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if thou hast not yet seen this outrageousness in its full extent, suppose it happening among men, and then thou wilt perceive the excess of the insolence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: As thus: should one approach thee who are a man, seeking to obtain mercy, and then, in the midst of his lying on the ground, should see an enemy, and leaving off to supplicate thee, begin to beat him; wouldest thou not make thyself more angry with him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This do thou consider as taking place with regard to God also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For so thou likewise, making supplication unto God, leavest thy supplication in the midst, and smitest thine enemy with thy words, and insultest the laws of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Him who made a law to dismiss all anger, thou art summoning against those that have vexed thee, and requiring Him to do things contrary to His own commandments.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Is it not enough for thee in the way of revenge, that thou thyself transgressest the law of God, but entreatest thou Him likewise to do so?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What? hath He forgotten what He commanded?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What? is He a man who spake these things?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It is God, who knows all things, and whose will is, that His own laws be kept with the utmost exactness, and who, so far from doing these things which thou art requiring of Him, doth even regard thee who sayest these things, merely because thou sayest them, with aversion and hatred, and exacts of thee the most extreme penalty.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How then seekest thou to obtain of Him things, from which He very seriously bids thee refrain?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet some there are, who have come to such a point of brutishness, as not only to make intercession against their enemies, but even to curse their children, and to taste, if only it might be, of their very flesh; or rather they are even tasting thereof.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For tell me not this, that thou hast not fixed thy teeth in the body of him that vexed thee; since thou hast done, at least as far as concerned thee, what is much more grievous; in claiming that wrath from above should fall upon him, and that he should be delivered over to undying punishment, and be overthrown with his whole house.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why, what sort of bites are as ferocious as this? what kind of weapons as bitter?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Not so did Christ instruct thee; not so did He command thee to stain thy mouth with blood.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, mouths made bloody with human flesh are not so shocking as tongues like these.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How then wilt thou salute thy brother? how wilt thou touch the sacrifice? how taste the Lord’s blood, when thou hast so much venom upon thy mind?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since when thou sayest, “Rend him in pieces, and overthrow his house, and destroy all,” when thou art imprecating on him ten thousand deaths, thou art in nothing different from a murderer, or rather from a wild beast that devours men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us cease then from this disease and madness, and that kindliness which He com manded let us show forth towards them that have vexed us: that we may become like “our Father which is in heaven.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And we shall cease therefrom, if we call to mind our own sins; if we strictly search out all our misdeeds at home, abroad, and in the market, and in church.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if for nothing else, surely for our disrespectfulness here we are worthy to undergo the utmost punishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when prophets are chanting, and apostles singing hymns, and God is discoursing, we wander without, and bring in upon us a turmoil of worldly business.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And we do not afford to the laws of God so great stillness, even as the spectators in the theatres to the emperor’s letters, keeping silence for them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For there, when these letters are being read, deputies at once, and governors, and senate, and people, stand all upright, with quietness hearkening to the words.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if amid that most profound silence any one should suddenly leap up and cry out, he suffers the utmost punishment, as having been insolent to the emperor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But here, when the letters from heaven are being read, great is the confusion on all sides.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet both He who sent the letters is much greater than this our king,and the assembly more venerable: for not men only, but angels too are in it; and these triumphs, of which the letters bear us the good tidings, are much more awful than those on earth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore not men only, but angels also and archangels; both the nations of heaven, and all we on the earth, are commanded to give praise.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, “Bless the Lord,” it is said, “all His works.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, for His are no small achievements, rather they surpass all speech, and thought, and understanding of man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And these things the prophets proclaim every day, each of them in a different way publishing this glorious triumph.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For one saith, “Thou hast gone up on high, Thou hast led captivity captive, and hast received gifts amongst men.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And, “The Lord strong and mighty in battle.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And another saith, “He shall divide the spoils of the strong.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed to this purpose He came, that He might “preach deliverance to captives, and recovery of sight to the blind.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And raising aloud the cry of victory over death, he said, “Where, O Death, is thy victory?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Where, O Grave, is thy sting?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And another again, declaring glad tidings of the most profound peace, said, “They shall beat their swords into ploughshares, and their spears into pruning hooks.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And while one calls on Jerusalem, saying, “Rejoice greatly, O daughter of Sion, for lo! thy King cometh to thee meek, riding upon an ass, and a young colt;”another proclaims His second coming also, saying on this wise, “The Lord, whom ye seek, will come, and who will abide the day of His coming?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Leap ye as calves set free from bonds.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And another again, amazed at such things, said, “This is our God; there shall none other be accounted of in comparison of Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet, nevertheless, while both these and many more sayings than these are being uttered, while we ought to tremble, and not so much as account ourselves to be on the earth; still, as though in the midst of a forum, we make an uproar and disturbance, and spend the whole time of our solemn assemblyin discoursing of things which are nothing to us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: When therefore both in little things, and in great, both in hearing, and in doing, both abroad, and at home, in the church, we are so negligent; and together with all this, pray also against our enemies: whence are we to have any hope of salvation, adding to so great sins yet another grievous enhancement, and equivalent to them all, even this unlawful prayer?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Have we then hereafter any right to marvel, if aught befall us of the things which are unexpected and painful? whereas we ought to marvel when no such thing befalls us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the former is in the natural order of things, but the latter were beyond all reason and expectation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For surely it is beyond reason, that they who are become enemies of God, and are provoking Him to anger, should enjoy sunshine and showers, and all the rest; who being men surpass the barbarity of wild beasts, setting themselves one against another, and by the biting of their neighbors staining their own tongues with blood: after the spiritual table, and His so great benefits, and His innumerable injunctions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore, considering these things, let us cast up that venom; let us put an end to our enmities, and let us make the prayers that become such as we are.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Instead of the brutality of devils, let us take upon us the mildness of angels; and in whatsoever things we may have been injured, let us, consider ing our own case, and the reward appointed us for this commandment, soften our anger; let us assuage the billows, that we may both pass through the present life calmly, and when we have departed thither, may find our Lord such as we have been towards our fellow-servants.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if this be a heavy and fearful thing, let us make it light and desirable; and let us open the glorious gates of confidence towards Him; and what we had not strength to effect by abstaining from sin, that let us accomplish by becoming gentle to them who have sinned against us (for this surely is not grievous, nor burdensome); and let us by doing kindnesses to our enemies, lay up beforehand much mercy for ourselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For so both during this present life all will love us, and above all others, God will both befriend and crown us, and will count us worthy of all the good things to come; unto which may we all attain, by the grace and love towards man of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be glory and might for ever and ever.
